snow_or_rain_notes: Snow patches on ground from mid-June to mid-July
Snow at Rola Kholi around mid-June
Snow on final ascent in early July
Snow closer to Bhrigu Lake in early August
temperature_notes: Mid-June to mid-July: Day 15-20°C, Night 1-5°C, 4 warm layers
Mid-July to mid-August: Day 15-20°C, Night 1-5°C, 3 warm layers
Mid-August to September: Day 12-15°C, Night 0-3°C, 4 warm layers

terrain_challenges: Uneven trails with daily ascents and descents
Extremely steep climb from Gulaba to Jonker Thatch gaining almost 3500 ft over 2 km
Continuous descent from Rola Kholi to Gulaba on the last day that is hard on the knees
Trail fades in meadows making it easy to get lost
Forest trail ahead of Gulaba gets mushy and difficult to climb in rain
altitude_or_weather_risks: Rapid elevation gain of more than 3500 ft from Gulaba to Jonker Thatch on day 1 increases susceptibility to Acute Mountain Sickness
Bhrigu Lake at 14,009 ft; above 10,000 ft AMS risk is real for first-time and experienced trekkers alike
Even fit trekkers climbing too high too soon are at equal risk of AMS
Rainfall or snowfall lasting hours can change trek dynamics
Rain can flood trails or make them slushy and unsafe

fitness_benchmark: You must be able to walk, jog or run 5 km in under 35 minutes. This is the minimum recommended fitness level that you must aim for.
approval_requirements: Make sure you upload these screenshots 15 to 20 days before your trek date. We want to see that you are being consistent in your preparation.
Your screenshots must demonstrate that you are meeting the fitness criteria for your trek.
Your runs don't meet your trek's fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month.
prep_window: 15 to 20 days before your trek date

experience_themes: Frozen lake surrounded by snow in early season
Lake melting in concentric circles as July progresses
Deep royal blue lake water in August
Flowers blooming around the lake in August
Lush green meadows emerging from mid-July onward
Cloud dance at Rola Kholi during peak monsoon
primary_watchouts: Heavy snow in May makes final ascent to lake difficult
Frequent rains and unannounced clouds during peak monsoon (mid-July to mid-August)
Landslides and roadblocks in monsoon can cause delays en route to base camp
Indiahikes monitors weather and trail conditions daily and may reroute, reschedule, or cancel treks
Trekkers visiting in May due to heavy snow blocking final ascent
Trekkers unable to buffer travel days around monsoon road disruptions
best_for: Trekkers seeking frozen lake and snow scenery (mid-June)
Trekkers wanting blooming flowers and deep blue lake (August)
Trekkers seeking lush meadows and clear mountain views (mid-August to September)
not_ideal_for: Trekkers visiting in May due to heavy snow blocking final ascent
Trekkers unable to buffer travel days around monsoon road disruptions
